questions.question: What is the main advantage of OFDM over single-carrier systems?

answers.c: Converts frequency-selective fading into flat fading per subcarrier
answers.d: Eliminates the need for channel coding

explanations.a: Incorrect. OFDM generally requires the same overall bandwidth as an equivalent single-carrier system; it simply divides that bandwidth into smaller segments.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Transmitter complexity in OFDM is actually higher due to the need for Inverse Fast Fourier Transform (IFFT) processing.
explanations.c: Correct. By dividing a wideband channel into many narrowband subcarriers, OFDM ensures each subcarrier experiences flat fading. This makes channel equalization at the receiver much simpler.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Channel coding is still absolutely necessary in OFDM to recover data lost to deep fades on specific subcarriers.

questions.question: Which of the following systems uses OFDM?

explanations.a: Incorrect. AM radio uses simple analog Amplitude Modulation.
explanations.b: Incorrect. 2G GSM cellular networks use Time Division Multiple Access (TDMA) and Frequency Division Multiple Access (FDMA).
explanations.c: Correct. Modern Wi-Fi standards rely heavily on OFDM to achieve high data rates and resist multipath interference in indoor environments.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Traditional CDMA (Code Division Multiple Access) uses spread-spectrum technology, not OFDM.

questions.question: What ensures orthogonality among subcarriers in OFDM?

answers.a: Using different modulation schemes per subcarrier
answers.c: Subcarriers are spaced at multiples of the inverse of the OFDM symbol duration

explanations.a: Incorrect. The modulation scheme (like QAM or BPSK) maps bits to symbols but has no physical effect on the mathematical orthogonality between different frequencies.
explanations.b: Incorrect. In OFDM, all subcarriers overlap and are transmitted simultaneously in the time domain.
explanations.c: Correct. Subcarriers are mathematically orthogonal (meaning they don't interfere with each other) only when their frequency spacing (Δf) is exactly equal to 1/Ts, where Ts is the active symbol duration.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Power allocation affects the Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR), not the orthogonality.

questions.question: The frequency spacing between subcarriers in OFDM is:

explanations.a: Incorrect. If spacing were arbitrary, subcarriers would interfere with one another, causing Inter-Carrier Interference (ICI).
explanations.b: Incorrect. The channel bandwidth is the total frequency range used by the system, which is divided among hundreds or thousands of subcarriers.
explanations.c: Correct. A spacing of exactly 1/Ts ensures that the integral of the product of any two distinct subcarriers over the symbol period is zero, which defines their orthogonality.
explanations.d: Incorrect. SNR defines signal quality, while subcarrier spacing is a fixed physical parameter of the system design.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to mitigate Inter-Symbol Interference (ISI) in OFDM?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Raised cosine filters are pulse-shaping filters commonly used to reduce ISI in single-carrier systems, but not in standard OFDM.
explanations.b: Correct. The Cyclic Prefix (CP) acts as a time-domain guard interval between OFDM symbols. It absorbs delayed multipath echoes from the previous symbol, preventing them from corrupting the current symbol.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Low-pass filters limit overall signal bandwidth but cannot resolve the time-delayed echoes that cause ISI.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Guard bands are empty frequency ranges at the edges of the spectrum used to prevent out-of-band interference, not time-domain ISI.

questions.question: OFDM is particularly effective in which type of channel?

explanations.a: Incorrect. While OFDM works in AWGN, its complex design is meant to solve issues far more complicated than simple white noise.
explanations.b: Correct. In a frequency-selective fading channel, a wideband signal gets heavily distorted. OFDM mitigates this by breaking the wideband channel into many narrow sub-bands that each experience easily-correctable flat fading.
explanations.c: Incorrect. A purely flat fading channel doesn't strictly require OFDM; a simpler single-carrier system could be equalized easily.
explanations.d: Incorrect. OFDM is actually quite sensitive to narrow-band interference and Doppler shifts.

questions.question: What is the role of the Inverse Fast Fourier Transform (IFFT) in an OFDM transmitter?

answers.a: Modulate data onto the carrier frequency
answers.b: Convert frequency-domain symbols into a time-domain waveform

explanations.a: Incorrect. Up-conversion to the actual high-frequency RF carrier is done later by analog RF mixers, not the IFFT.
explanations.b: Correct. The IFFT takes the parallel array of frequency-domain QAM/BPSK symbols and mathematically transforms them into a single, combined time-domain OFDM waveform for transmission.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Filtering is handled separately by digital and analog filters.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Demodulation (converting time-domain back to frequency-domain) is performed by the FFT at the receiver.

questions.question: In OFDM, the cyclic prefix should be:

answers.b: Longer than the entire OFDM frame
answers.c: Longer than the channel delay spread
answers.d: Equal to the number of subcarriers

explanations.a: Incorrect. If the CP equaled the symbol duration, spectral efficiency would drop by 50% because half the transmission time would be redundant data.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The CP is appended to each individual symbol within the frame, making it much shorter than a full frame.
explanations.c: Correct. To completely eliminate Inter-Symbol Interference (ISI), the time length of the Cyclic Prefix must be greater than or equal to the maximum delay spread (the longest echo) of the multipath channel.
explanations.d: Incorrect. The CP is measured in units of time (or time-domain sample counts), not by the number of frequency subcarriers.

questions.question: What type of equalizer is commonly used in OFDM systems?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Complex time-domain FIR filters are used in single-carrier systems; OFDM specifically aims to avoid using them.
explanations.b: Correct. Because each OFDM subcarrier experiences simple flat fading, the receiver only needs to perform a single complex multiplication (a scalar equalizer) per subcarrier in the frequency domain.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Adaptive time-domain equalizers (like LMS) are computationally heavy and typically used in wideband single-carrier systems.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Decision Feedback Equalization (DFE) is a complex technique used for severe ISI, which OFDM handles natively via the Cyclic Prefix.
